en.extract:
The melodious lark (Mirafra cheniana) is a species of lark in the family Alaudidae found in southern Africa. It is currently threatened by habitat loss.


Taxonomy and systematics
The alternate name of singing bush lark usually refers to the species of that name, Mirafra cantillans. Other alternate names for the melodious lark include: Latakoo lark, Latakoo bush lark, melodious bushlark, singing bush lark, Southern lark, Southern singing bush lark and Southern singing lark.


Distribution and habitat
The melodious lark has a probable maximum range of 110,400 square kilometres (42,600 sq mi), separated into many disjunct populations located in South Africa (Eastern Cape, the Free State, Gauteng, KwaZulu-Natal, Limpopo, and North West Province), Botswana and Zimbabwe. At times, local populations will abandon one area for another when the regular dry-season fires occur.


Habitat
The natural habitat of the melodious lark is subtropical or tropical, seasonally wet or flooded, lowland grassland. Within these regions, it prefers the drier slopes, especially in open runs between grassy tussocks.
Melodious larks select different textures of grass or grass parts to build their domed, obliquely-accessed nests. They use the harder and more fibrous grasses and stalks for an outer thatch, while the finer and softer grasses or grass leaves are used to line the nest's interior.


Behaviour and ecology


Breeding
The melodious lark is assessed as likely to be both territorial and monogamous, and lays a clutch of 2-4 eggs. It is generally resident, breeding from September to March. The level of breeding activity varies depending on locality. The most active period in South Africa is between November and January, while in Zimbabwe it is from January to March.


Food and feeding
The melodious lark forages on the ground for food, eating mostly grass seeds supplemented with insects.


References


External links

Melodious lark - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.

The '''melodious lark''' ('''''Mirafra cheniana''''') is a species of [[lark]] in the family [[Alaudidae]] found in southern Africa. It is currently threatened by [[habitat loss]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The alternate name of '''singing bush lark''' usually refers to the species of that name, ''[[Mirafra cantillans]]''. Other alternate names for the melodious lark include: '''Latakoo lark''', '''Latakoo bush lark''', '''melodious bushlark''', '''singing bush lark''', '''Southern lark''', '''Southern singing bush lark''' and '''Southern singing lark'''.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=AB507233B804883A|title=Mirafra cheniana - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2016-12-10}}</ref>

== Distribution and habitat ==
The melodious lark has a probable maximum [[range (biology)|range]] of {{convert|110,400|km2}}, separated into many disjunct populations located in South Africa ([[Eastern Cape]], the [[Free State (South African province)|Free State]], [[Gauteng]], [[KwaZulu-Natal]], [[Limpopo]], and [[North West Province (South Africa)|North West Province]]), Botswana and Zimbabwe.<ref name=bi>{{cite web |url=http://www.birdlife.org/datazone/species/index.html?action=SpcHTMDetails.asp&sid=8095&m=0 |title=''Melodious Lark - BirdLife Species Factsheet'' |author=Compilers: Andrew O'Brien, Joe Taylor |work=Evaluators: Stuart Butchart, Joe Taylor|publisher=[[BirdLife International]] |year=2008 |access-date=May 11, 2009}}</ref> At times, local populations will abandon one area for another when the regular dry-season fires occur.<ref name=bi/>

=== Habitat ===
The natural [[habitat]] of the melodious lark is subtropical or tropical, seasonally wet or flooded, lowland [[grassland]].<ref name=bi/> Within these regions, it prefers the drier slopes, especially in open runs between grassy [[tussock (grass)|tussock]]s.

Melodious larks select different textures of grass or grass parts to build their domed, obliquely-accessed nests. They use the harder and more fibrous grasses and stalks for an outer thatch, while the finer and softer grasses or grass leaves are used to line the nest's interior.<ref name=bi/>

== Behaviour and ecology ==
=== Breeding ===
The melodious lark is assessed as likely to be both [[Territory (animal)|territorial]] and [[monogamy in animals|monogamous]], and lays a clutch of 2-4 eggs.<ref name=bi/> It is generally resident, breeding from September to March. The level of breeding activity varies depending on locality. The most active period in South Africa is between November and January, while in Zimbabwe it is from January to March.<ref name=bi/>

=== Food and feeding ===
The melodious lark forages on the ground for food, eating mostly grass seeds supplemented with insects.<ref name=bi/>

zh.extract:
南非歌百灵（学名：Mirafra cheniana），是百灵科歌百灵属的一种，分布于博茨瓦纳、津巴布韦和南非。全球活动范围约为210,000平方千米。该物种的保护状况被评为近危。
南非歌百灵的栖息地为亚热带或热带的（低地）干草原。


参考文献
Mirafra cheniana. BirdLife.org.   [2011-06-27]. （原始内容存档于2016-03-04）.
